items.translated_title: China’s top chip foundry SMIC unveils action plan for seizing new growth opportunities
items.content:
Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp (SMIC) launched an action plan to enhance its current businesses and seek new growth in 2026, as the largest contract chipmaker in China aims to solidify its role as the backbone of the country’s self-sufficiency drive.
Released alongside its annual report for 2025, the plan outlined a commitment to “optimising existing stock and digging for new increments”, the company said in a stock exchange filing on Thursday.
The company said two trends would...

items.title: Rewriting Commerce For The Agentic Age
items.creator: Jolene Otremba
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Rewriting Commerce For The Agentic Age
items.content:
For Gary Liu, the most profound shift in commerce today is not about faster checkouts or new wallets. It is about who, or what, sits between merchants and consumers.
“There’s only one thing that’s fair to everyone - you only have 24 hours a day,” says Liu, CEO of Antom and Senior Vice President of Ant International. “Whichever service occupies the most consumer time, that’s where commerce should be.”
Increasingly, that time is being spent not browsing websites, but interacting with AI...

items.title: Greater Bay Area trails peers in basic research despite strong tech potential: Deloitte
items.creator: Mia Nurmamat
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Greater Bay Area trails peers in basic research despite strong tech potential: Deloitte

images.alt: The GBA has emerged as one of the country’s most important tech hubs. Photo: Edmond So
images.title: The GBA has emerged as one of the country’s most important tech hubs. Photo: Edmond So

items.content:
The Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macau Greater Bay Area (GBA) is lagging behind China’s national average in basic research, despite showing strong potential in sectors such as semiconductors and smart manufacturing, according to Deloitte.
The cluster of cities in southern China, which included Shenzhen, spent about 28.9 billion yuan (US$4.2 billion) on basic research in 2024, accounting for just 5.67 per cent of total spending in research and development (R&D), the consulting firm said in a report...

items.title: Google’s TurboQuant AI advance dents memory-chip stocks, but analysts say ‘buy the dip’
items.creator: Vincent Chow
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Google’s TurboQuant AI advance dents memory-chip stocks, but analysts say ‘buy the dip’
items.content:
A new artificial intelligence algorithm developed by Google that could reduce demand for memory chips triggered a slump in global memory stocks, but analysts said it presented an opportunity for investors to “buy the dip”.
Shares in memory giants including Samsung and SK Hynix fell after Google said in a blog post on Tuesday that the algorithm, called TurboQuant, reduced the memory demands of key-value (KV) caches – a crucial component of how AI models are served to users – by six times through...

items.title: DJI unveils its first 360-degree drone amid patent dispute with crosstown rival Insta360
items.creator: Coco Feng
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: DJI unveils its first 360-degree drone amid patent dispute with crosstown rival Insta360

images.alt: The DJI logo is seen on a mobile phone screen in front of the company’s website. Photo: Shutterstock Images
images.title: The DJI logo is seen on a mobile phone screen in front of the company’s website. Photo: Shutterstock Images

items.content:
China’s DJI announced its first 360-degree drone on Thursday, directly challenging domestic rival Insta360 in a nascent market segment that is now at the centre of an escalating patent war.
The new product, the DJI Avata 360, marks a significant expansion for the company beyond traditional aerial photography. The drone features a front-mounted omnidirectional camera capable of capturing high dynamic range (HDR) images at 8K resolution and 60 frames per second, according to official...

items.title: AI rift widens as China urges boycott of top US conference over sanctions ban
items.creator: Vincent Chow
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: AI rift widens as China urges boycott of top US conference over sanctions ban
items.content:
Chinese computer scientists and researchers have been urged to boycott a major artificial intelligence conference after its organisers barred submissions from US-sanctioned institutions, including leading Chinese tech groups such as Huawei Technologies.
The move by the China Computer Federation (CCF) is the latest flashpoint in deepening US-China tensions over AI, a fast-evolving field with far-reaching economic, social and military implications.
The influential professional body said on...

items.title: Meituan posts second consecutive quarterly loss amid costly instant commerce battle
items.creator: Ben Jiang,Ann Cao
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Meituan posts second consecutive quarterly loss amid costly instant commerce battle
items.content:
Chinese food and on-demand delivery giant Meituan reported on Thursday its second consecutive quarterly loss since 2022, as intense competition and a prolonged price war with Alibaba Group Holding and JD.com last year squeezed margins.
The company reported a 15 billion yuan (US$2.2 billion) adjusted net loss – worse than the 12.9 billion yuan average estimate by analysts – for the fourth quarter ended December 31, compared with adjusted net profit of 9.8 billion yuan a year earlier.
Its...

items.title: China’s Kuaishou slumps as analysts flag growth pressure despite strong results
items.creator: Ben Jiang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s Kuaishou slumps as analysts flag growth pressure despite strong results
items.content:
Shares of Chinese short-video platform Kuaishou fell more than 13 per cent to HK$45.96 by midday in Hong Kong on Thursday, a day after reporting strong sales growth, as analysts warned that its core advertising and live-streaming businesses could face mounting pressure this year.
The sharp decline reflected investor concerns over a cautious growth outlook for the Beijing-based firm, compounded by its heavy spending on artificial intelligence, according to Zhang Xueru, an analyst at investment...

items.title: China’s weight-loss drug makers take on global giants as Novo Nordisk patent expires
items.creator: Julie Zhang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s weight-loss drug makers take on global giants as Novo Nordisk patent expires
items.content:
At least 10 weight-loss injections and oral pills are lining up for regulatory approval in China, in a market projected to reach about US$14 billion by 2030.
Novo Nordisk’s blockbuster semaglutide, which generated about US$35 billion in global revenue last year, lost patent protection in China on March 20, clearing the way for rivals.
The rush comes as China’s drug regulator accelerates approvals of innovative therapies, helped by a surge in out-licensing deals with global pharmaceutical...

items.title: Euro, Chinese yuan to end US dollar dominance, top economist says
items.creator: Kandy Wong
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Euro, Chinese yuan to end US dollar dominance, top economist says
items.content:
Professor Kenneth Rogoff of Harvard University has repeatedly warned that the US dollar is approaching a crisis of legitimacy. Having written extensively on the global recession in the late 2000s, Rogoff has turned his focus to the US currency’s increasingly unstable place at the top of the world’s financial hierarchy. A former chief economist at the International Monetary Fund and a chess grandmaster, he published Our Dollar, Your Problem in May last year.
In this interview, Rogoff elaborates...

items.title: Why China’s humanoid robots are still waiting for their ‘ChatGPT moment’
items.creator: Vincent Chow
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Why China’s humanoid robots are still waiting for their ‘ChatGPT moment’

images.alt: A Kuavo-5W humanoid robot by Leju Robotics passes freshly made coffee to a journalist during a demonstration at the 2026 Zhongguancun Forum, a major annual technology conference in Beijing on March 25. Photo: Reuters
images.title: A Kuavo-5W humanoid robot by Leju Robotics passes freshly made coffee to a journalist during a demonstration at the 2026 Zhongguancun Forum, a major annual technology conference in Beijing on March 25. Photo: Reuters

items.content:
A “ChatGPT moment” for China’s humanoid robots – the tipping point at which the technology becomes widely usable – remains years away as persistent challenges in adapting to new tasks and training efficiency continue to hold back the industry, leading experts said on Wednesday at the Boao Forum for Asia in Hainan.
Despite rapid advances in recent years, humanoid robots were still far from large-scale deployment, with both hardware and software limitations yet to be fully resolved, panellists...

items.title: Semicon China: AI, advanced packaging set to drive country’s chip industry growth
items.creator: Ann Cao
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Semicon China: AI, advanced packaging set to drive country’s chip industry growth
items.content:
Agentic artificial intelligence and advanced packaging technology will be key growth drivers for China’s chip industry in coming years, as the country is expected to increase its share of chipmaking capacity to nearly half of the world’s total by 2028.
According to data revealed at Semicon China, the world’s largest chip industry trade show, China’s share of wafer fabrication capacity for mainstream processes was expected to reach 42 per cent of global capacity by 2028.
That would mark a rapid...

items.title: Pinduoduo operator’s profit slides as Chinese firm cites higher reinvestment
items.creator: Wency Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Pinduoduo operator’s profit slides as Chinese firm cites higher reinvestment
items.content:
PDD Holdings, the Chinese e-commerce group behind Pinduoduo and global budget marketplace Temu, on Wednesday reported an 11 per cent drop in quarterly profit amid higher sales as the company continued a pivot towards greater reinvestment.
Net profit for the quarter fell to 24.5 billion yuan (US$3.6 billion), missing a consensus analyst estimate of 29.1 billion yuan. Meanwhile, revenue rose 12 per cent from a year earlier to 123.9 billion yuan, aligning with a consensus analyst estimate of 123.7...

items.title: China Mobile bets on Hong Kong as gateway for global computing flows
items.creator: Mia Nurmamat
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China Mobile bets on Hong Kong as gateway for global computing flows
items.content:
China Mobile has invested nearly HK$10 billion (US$1.28 billion) over five years to help turn Hong Kong into a global computing hub, integrating the city into China’s national network.
The world’s largest telecoms operator by subscribers on Wednesday opened a new data centre in northern Hong Kong and said it would step up investment in next-generation submarine cables.
The state-owned group aims to bring Hong Kong into China’s national computing network, which has the world’s second-largest...

items.title: China names Beijing, other city clusters as global tech hubs in self-reliance push
items.creator: Ben Jiang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China names Beijing, other city clusters as global tech hubs in self-reliance push
items.content:
China is stepping up its efforts to build Beijing and its environs, along with other major city clusters, into “international technological innovation centres”, according to a high-ranking official – part of the country’s accelerated push for tech self-sufficiency.
Speaking at the opening of the state-backed Zhongguancun Forum in Beijing on Wednesday, Chinese Vice-Premier Ding Xuexiang said the country would intensify its campaign to achieve “high-level self-reliance in science and technology”...

items.title: Shenzhen eyes ‘leapfrog’ gains in AI server supply chain under self-reliance push

items.translated_title: Shenzhen eyes ‘leapfrog’ gains in AI server supply chain under self-reliance push

images.alt: The Shenzhen plan supports the “deep integration” of the open-source RISC-V architecture. Photo: Handout
images.title: The Shenzhen plan supports the “deep integration” of the open-source RISC-V architecture. Photo: Handout

items.content:
Shenzhen – southern China’s technology powerhouse – has launched an ambitious three-year action plan to build itself into a hub for intelligent computing clusters by advancing key areas such as semiconductors, storage and AI servers.
The city aimed to see a “leapfrog” increase in the production capacity and shipment volume of the entire AI server supply chain by 2028, according to the plan published Monday by Shenzhen’s industry and information technology bureau.
The blueprint is geared to...

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Hong Kong stocks extend rebound on signs of diplomatic path in Iran conflict
items.content:
Hong Kong stocks extended their rebound on Wednesday on signs that a diplomatic solution to Middle East tensions was making progress.
The Hang Seng Index rose 1.1 per cent to 25,335.95 at the close, adding to a 2.7 per cent gain a day earlier. Technology stocks advanced, driving the Hang Seng Tech Index up by 1.9 per cent, after China’s anti-monopoly regulator called for an end to the price war in the on-demand food delivery market.
On the mainland, the CSI 300 Index climbed 1.4 per cent and the...

items.title: China’s WeRide eyes Hong Kong, Singapore roads for robotaxis as self-driving giants expand

items.translated_title: China’s WeRide eyes Hong Kong, Singapore roads for robotaxis as self-driving giants expand
items.content:
Chinese self-driving technology developers continue to expand outside the mainland as WeRide seeks to launch robotaxi services this year in Hong Kong and Singapore, according to an executive.
WeRide’s planned entry into Hong Kong was set to cover both robotaxis and robobuses, said senior director of public relations and marketing Maeve Zhang in a media briefing on Tuesday, without disclosing the operation areas or a launch date.
Meanwhile, the Guangzhou-based company said it planned to launch...

items.title: US move to ban Chinese routers as part of onshoring push could be uphill battle: analysts

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: US move to ban Chinese routers as part of onshoring push could be uphill battle: analysts
items.content:
A US move to ban imports of new foreign-made consumer routers aims to pressure Chinese manufacturers to “onshore” parts of their production chain, although that will be a challenge given limited US manufacturing capacity, according to analysts.
The US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) said on Monday that it had updated its Covered List to include consumer-grade routers produced in foreign countries, citing concerns that the devices could “pose unacceptable risks to the national security of...

items.translated_title: Alibaba debuts its latest RISC-V-based chip amid shift to AI agents

images.alt: Alibaba was an early mover in China’s RISC-V space, launching the XuanTie series in 2018. Photo: Douyin
images.title: Alibaba was an early mover in China’s RISC-V space, launching the XuanTie series in 2018. Photo: Douyin

items.content:
Alibaba Group Holding’s research arm Damo Academy has unveiled a chip designed to fuel artificial intelligence agents, as the tech giant bets on open-source RISC-V architecture to ride the wave of agentic AI.
XuanTie C950, the latest flagship in Alibaba’s XuanTie RISC-V series, was introduced at the company’s annual ecosystem conference in Shanghai on Tuesday. Designed for high-performance tasks in cloud computing and AI computing, the C950 is a “CPU core” – the fundamental architecture for a...
